name: Pipedrive
description: A sales-focused CRM designed specifically for small sales teams. Pipedrive's visual pipeline management and activity-based selling approach make it popular with startups and SMBs who want simplicity without sacrificing pipeline visibility.
whyUseIt: Pipedrive is built by salespeople for salespeople. Its visual pipeline — where you drag deals between stages — is intuitive and keeps your focus on next actions. If Salesforce feels overwhelming and HubSpot feels too marketing-heavy, Pipedrive is the sweet spot for pure sales teams under 50 people.
aiCapabilities: Pipedrive's AI Sales Assistant analyses your pipeline and suggests actions: which deals to focus on, when to follow up, and where deals are at risk of stalling.
funnelPosition: Sales pipeline — from qualified lead through to close
sellerWorkflow: AEs manage deals on the visual pipeline board, set activities (calls, emails, meetings) for each deal, and track progress. The activity-based approach keeps focus on next steps rather than deal status.
managerExpects: Every deal has a scheduled next activity. Pipeline stages reflect reality. Activity velocity is consistent — no deals sitting idle without next steps.
demoDescription: View the visual pipeline, drag a deal to a new stage, schedule a next activity, send a tracked email, and review the AI assistant's recommendations for pipeline improvement.

vocabulary.definition: A task tied to a deal: call, email, meeting, deadline. Pipedrive's core philosophy is that you can't control outcomes, but you can control activities.
A deal that has been idle for too long without activity. Pipedrive highlights these automatically to prevent pipeline rot.

beginnerMistakes: Letting deals rot without scheduled next activities
Not customising pipeline stages to match your actual sales process
Forgetting to log activities — Pipedrive's value comes from consistent usage
